subject: Public Health
title: Water: The Forgotten Nutrient
description: A 60-second lesson on Water: The Forgotten Nutrient.

hook.text: You can survive weeks without food but only days without water. It's the most essential nutrient.

buildup.text: Water regulates temperature, transports nutrients, removes waste, and cushions every organ.

discovery.text: Even mild dehydration—just 1-2% body water loss—impairs concentration and physical performance.

twist.text: 2.2 billion people lack safely managed drinking water—dehydration and waterborne diseases intertwine.

climax.text: Clean water access is both a nutrition issue and an infectious disease issue—they cannot be separated.

punchline.text: The most vital nutrient flows from a tap—if you're lucky.

quiz.question: How many people lack safely managed drinking water?
